source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A large rectangle is partitioned into four rectangles by two segments parallel to its sides. The areas of three of the resulting rectangles are shown. What is the area of the fourth rectangle?
<image1>

image_dependency: question asks for the area of a fourth rectangle where the three given areas are only described as 'shown', so the needed values/layout come from the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is primarily visual
the task depends on the spatial partition of one rectangle into four sub-rectangles by side-parallel segments, which is a diagram-based relation
multi_step: solving requires identifying how the four sub-rectangles relate through shared side lengths in the partitioned diagram
the answer is not read directly; it requires combining the three shown areas to infer the missing fourth area
this is a short but genuine multi-step geometry/algebra reasoning problem rather than single-step lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='15', so the target format is precise
for this standard rectangle-partition setup, the inferred missing area can be checked by consistent side-length/area relations
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and both image and answer are present, supporting downstream verification

decision_reasons: 题干、图像和数值答案均已具备，核心语义资产完整
图像依赖高但对应图片存在，题干中省略的关键数值可由图像恢复
可验证性高且答案为明确数值，适合进入候选池
虽有 visual_values_omitted_from_question_text 风险标记，但不足以影响核心语义获取
